comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (round 3, --ref HEAD). Advisory triage: added test_non_claim_edge_leaves_claimed_session_untouched (T8 non-claim edge). Deferred/justified: E1 consumer .get() contract + T8 claim_result.schema + T11 rollback-snapshot — no new consumer/claim-output code lands in S2 (read surface + compaction round-trip are S3/199b); claim_result reflects the claim OPERATION, not full compiled state; tests already read via .get(). Not re-editing the ticket to preserve the fresh attestation.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The core deliverable of the epic: an `in_progress` ticket should carry the coding-agent
session that STARTED it, end-to-end (written on the STATUS event AND folded into compiled
state), additively — without disturbing `assignee` / `author`, and byte-identical to today
when no session id is present. Recording without folding produces no observable outcome, so
both halves ship together.

## What
- **Write (`src/rebar/_commands/txn.py`).** In `claim_core` and `transition_core`, when the
  event is an `open -> in_progress` STATUS, call the shared `resolve_session_id()` (story
  6014) via a small `_stamp_session(status_data)` helper; when it returns non-None, add
  `data["session"]`. When None, OMIT the key entirely so the event bytes are identical to the
  pre-feature path. Stamp on ANY `open -> in_progress` STATUS — the `claim` command AND a
  bare `transition <id> open in_progress` (including the parent-first cascade) — not just
  `claim`.
- **Fold (`src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py`).** In `process_status`, a
  `_fold_claimed_session(state, data)` helper sets `state["claimed_session"] = data.get("session")`
  on the `open -> in_progress` edge (`current_status == "open" and status == "in_progress"`).
  It is invoked ONLY in the two branches that APPLY this event's status — the normal-update
  branch and the fork-WINNER branch — and NEVER in the branch where the existing chain wins,
  so a losing concurrent claim never overwrites the winner's session (epic advisory G6/T8).
  The edge condition reads the **incoming event's** `data.get("current_status")` and
  `data.get("status")` — NOT the post-update `state["status"]` (already mutated to
  `in_progress` by the time the helper runs) — so in the fork-winner branch it still keys on
  the event's declared `open -> in_progress` edge, never firing for a `<other> -> in_progress`
  winner (advisory T8).
  `claimed_session` records the session of the CURRENT claim episode (mirrors `assignee`): a
  session-less re-claim folds `None`, deliberately CLEARING any stale prior id so the field
  never mis-attributes the current in_progress slot to a past session (advisory T9). A later
  non-claim edge (e.g. `blocked -> in_progress`) leaves it untouched.
- **Initial-state default (`src/rebar/reducer/_state.py`).** Add `claimed_session: None` to
  `make_initial_state()` (mirrors `assignee`), so a fresh event-replay always carries the key
  (value `None`) rather than key-absent.
- **Compiled-state schema (`src/rebar/schemas/ticket_state.schema.json`).** Add a
  `claimed_session: {type: [string, null]}` property beside `assignee` so the new public
  compiled-state field is enumerated in the schema.
- **Interim key-presence contract (until S3).** A snapshot persisted BEFORE this stack lacks
  `claimed_session` entirely, so during the coexistence window a snapshot-served state may be
  key-absent while a fresh-replay state is `None`. Consumers MUST therefore read via
  `state.get("claimed_session")` (tolerating both); S3 (199b) adds the snapshot-restore default
  + compaction round-trip that closes the window. This story does not claim key-presence for
  pre-existing snapshots.
- **Forward/back-compat.** `data["session"]` is an additive data key; a reducer build without
  the fold ignores unknown data keys (it only reads `data.get("status")` / `current_status`),
  so an older clone tolerates the event. Absent-session events are byte-identical to today.
- **Dependency.** The shared `resolve_session_id()` is story 6014, landed as the BASE COMMIT
  of this stacked change series; this story is plan-reviewed with `--ref HEAD` so the review
  snapshot includes it.

## Scope
- IN: the write stamp (both cores) + the reducer fold + the `make_initial_state` default +
  the `ticket_state.schema.json` property + unit/integration tests (present,
  absent/byte-identical, bare-transition, fork-winner gating, forward-compat tolerance,
  session-less stale-clear, initial-state default).
- OUT: snapshot/compaction default + pretty read surface + MCP outputSchema (S3 / 199b);
  `OPENCODE_SESSION_ID`/`CODEX_THREAD_ID` + harness provenance (S5 / c557); capture hooks
  (S4 / S6). The shared resolver itself is story 6014 (already landed as this stack's base).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Claiming with a session env var set records `claimed_session` in compiled state.
      Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_claimed_session.py -k claim_records -q`.
- [ ] Claiming with NO session env var set folds `claimed_session` to `None` (key-present,
      consistent with the initial-state default) AND the written STATUS event carries no
      `session` key (byte-identical no-session event path). Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_claimed_session.py -k absent -q` asserts
      `state["claimed_session"] is None` AND `"session" not in` the STATUS event's `data`.
- [ ] A bare `transition <id> open in_progress` records it too (cascade parents included).
      Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_claimed_session.py -k transition -q`.
- [ ] Fork-winner gating: when two competing `open -> in_progress` STATUS events carry
      DIFFERENT session ids, compiled `claimed_session` equals the lexical-UUID WINNER's
      session (never the loser's). Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_claimed_session.py -k fork -q` replays both events in BOTH
      orders and asserts the winner's session each time.
- [ ] Forward-compat: `process_status` folds a STATUS whose `data` carries both `session` and
      an arbitrary UNKNOWN extra key without error, and the unknown key does not enter state
      (proxy for an older clone tolerating the additive key). Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_claimed_session.py -k forward_compat -q`.
- [ ] Stale-clear (T9): a later `open -> in_progress` claim carrying NO session folds
      `claimed_session` back to `None` (never leaves a stale prior id). Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_claimed_session.py -k session_less_reclaim -q`.
- [ ] `make_initial_state()` defaults `claimed_session` to `None` (key-present). Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_claimed_session.py -k initial_state -q`.
- [ ] `ticket_state.schema.json` enumerates `claimed_session` as `[string, null]`. Proof:
      `grep -n '"claimed_session"' src/rebar/schemas/ticket_state.schema.json`.

## Depends on
- S1 (shared resolver `resolve_session_id`) — landed as the base of this stack.
compiled_state.title: Record the claiming session id on open->in_progress (claim + bare transition)
